Making a Text Editor

I have been planning on craeting a user based project for quite a while. Altough it is not my greates goal in life to make apps for people, but the number of utilities and unknowns surrounding text editors always felt mysterious. I had used nano for a while, some of my professors used vi (not vim or nvim, just vi) and I had found nvim attractive from all the ramble about it on reddit. So I did some research and found snaptoken’s text editor tutorial, and I decided to give it a go. And though I did start to remake kilo following his steps my interest faded over a couple of days (I was going slow, ok?), and decided to drop it.

Recently, I gained back the interest to continue (this is something that happens to me when a lot of work accumulates, I take on more projects). But I was planning on doing it on Rust, but that idea didn’t work as well. I had just picked up C, and now kilo was not gibberish anymore. So, I decided to use all tools available to start a project like that on my own. I will try documenting the process, as long as it may take.

Part 1 - Raw Mode the easy way.

kilo tutorial does basicly everything by hand. I don’t care enough to re-invent the wheel, I’d rather focus on how the editor will be used, what kind of features will it have. right now I am working on this part, as easy as it may sound Im not married to this project and will probably procrastinate until its finished somewhere in the year three thousand.
